questions: How do I spot a scam?
What should I do if I've been scammed?
answers: Common red flags include too-good-to-be-true offers, urgent pressure to act fast, requests for personal info or money, and unfamiliar sender addresses or phone numbers.
Scam chinar upay — jodi offer ta onek beshi bhalo laagey, taratari decision nite bole, personal info chaay, ba unknown source theke aashey tahole savdhan.
Common online scams include phishing emails, fake lottery wins, romance scams, tech support scams, investment fraud, and fake e-commerce sites.
If you've been scammed, immediately contact your bank, change your passwords, report it to local authorities and the platform where it happened, and warn others.
